topic: Someone discovers their neighbor is secretly wealthy
raw_output:
[story 1]

The incessant drone of Mr. Henderson’s imported lawnmower, starting precisely at seven in the morning, was the first thing that set Clara’s teeth on edge. She kept her windows tightly shut, the faint, rhythmic thrum vibrating through the cheap plaster of her living room. Every time he started up, a low, simmering tension would coil in her stomach, making her jaw clench. Today, however, something was different. As the engine sputtered to life, Clara caught a glimpse of a gleaming, obsidian-black sedan pulling out of the driveway, a vehicle so polished it seemed to absorb the morning light. She squinted, trying to reconcile the image with the familiar, slightly overgrown suburban street. A cold knot tightened beneath her ribs.

[story 2]

"Honestly, the sheer audacity of that noise," Mark muttered, rubbing his temples as he stared out his kitchen window. He kept glancing toward the manicured property next door, a place that seemed impossibly perfect against the backdrop of their slightly crumbling duplex. He hated the way the sunlight seemed to catch the immaculate hedges, the way the sheer scale of the landscaping suggested resources far beyond their modest means. He shifted uncomfortably on the sofa, the fabric of the cushion digging into his back. He’d been trying to ignore the fact that Mr. Henderson, the quiet man who always kept to himself, was likely funding that level of upkeep. The feeling was a tight, unpleasant pressure behind his eyes.

[story 3]

I slammed the refrigerator door shut, the sudden noise echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. I couldn't stand the way the silence felt charged, heavy with unspoken judgment. It had been weeks of noticing the subtle discrepancies: the pristine, almost unnatural condition of their neighbor’s garden, the silent, expensive-looking deliveries that never seemed to require a signature, and the sheer, unearned ease with which they navigated their property. When I saw the valet pull up—a silver chariot that looked like it belonged in a magazine—I felt a distinct tightening in my chest, a sharp, unwelcome sensation that demanded I look away immediately.
